skill.name: arxiv-search
skill.description: Search arXiv for scholarly preprints across computer science, physics, mathematics, statistics, quantitative biology, and related fields.
skill.summary: Find relevant arXiv papers and return titles, summaries, and direct paper links.
skill.author: DougTrajano

security_audit.summary: Static weak cryptography findings are false positives: the cited lines contain descriptive text or argparse metadata, not cryptographic code. The external command findings are mostly markdown code formatting and a documented pip install command, while the real publish-time concern is legitimate network access to arXiv through a Python script.

medium_findings.title: Intentional Network Access to arXiv
medium_findings.description: The Python script imports the arxiv package, creates an arxiv.Client, and retrieves results from arXiv based on the user query. This is the stated purpose of the skill and no credential exfiltration was found, but it requires outbound network access to an external scholarly service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly constructs an arXiv client and iterates over remote search results. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but it is still an external network dependency.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain the skill description, descriptive arXiv background text, and an argparse description string. I found no hash, cipher, password handling, or cryptographic implementation at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced code and markdown lines are plain descriptions and CLI metadata. There is no cryptographic API usage or security-sensitive algorithm to evaluate.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported SKILL.md locations are markdown backticks, run_skill_script examples, and a pip install instruction. They do not execute automatically inside the skill, but users should review dependencies before installing the arxiv package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is instructional markdown and does not contain shell execution logic. The only real command is a user-run package installation instruction.

low_findings.title: Unbounded Result Count May Increase External Requests
low_findings.description: The --max-papers argument accepts any integer and is passed to arxiv.Search as max_results. A very large value could create excessive external requests or slow execution, although no command injection or data exfiltration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The CLI does not enforce an upper bound before passing max_papers to the search call. The impact depends on arXiv package behavior and runtime limits.

content.value_statement: Researchers need fast access to relevant preprints across technical fields. This skill searches arXiv and returns concise paper titles, summaries, and direct links.
actual_capabilities: Searches arXiv with a user-provided query string.
Limits returned papers with the --max-papers option.
Sorts results by arXiv relevance ranking.
Returns each paper title, abstract summary, and arXiv URL.
Reports a clear error if the arxiv Python package is missing.
limitations: Requires the separate arxiv Python package to be installed.
Only searches arXiv and does not query PubMed, Crossref, or Google Scholar.
Returns preprints that may not be peer reviewed.
Does not rank papers by citation count, venue quality, or publication status.

use_cases.title: Track New Machine Learning Papers
use_cases.description: Find arXiv preprints for a model family, method, or benchmark before journal publication.

use_cases.title: Survey Computational Biology Methods
use_cases.description: Search for papers that connect biological datasets with algorithms, statistics, or predictive modeling.

use_cases.title: Prepare Technical Literature Reviews
use_cases.description: Collect candidate papers with summaries and links for an early review or project brief.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Paper Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Find a small set of papers on one topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search arXiv for papers about protein folding prediction and return the top 5 results.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Research Directions
prompt_templates.prompt: Search arXiv for diffusion models in medical imaging. Summarize the most relevant themes from the top 10 results.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Reading List
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a focused starting list for a project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find arXiv papers on single cell RNA-seq foundation models. Group the results by method type and suggest a reading order.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Literature Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: Filter papers for a specific research decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search arXiv for graph neural networks for protein interaction prediction. Identify papers that seem most relevant for a benchmark design.

output_examples.input: Search for transformer models in protein design with 5 papers.
output_examples.output: A short list of arXiv papers with each title, abstract summary, and direct arXiv link.

output_examples.input: Find recent work on diffusion models for molecule generation.
output_examples.output: Relevant arXiv results sorted by arXiv relevance, suitable for follow-up reading and comparison.

output_examples.input: Search for Bayesian optimization in synthetic biology.
output_examples.output: Paper summaries and links that help identify methods, datasets, and possible review candidates.

best_practices: Use specific technical terms, model names, datasets, or arXiv subject areas in the query.
Start with a small result limit, then expand after reviewing the first results.
Verify important claims in the paper PDF or official publication record before citing.
anti_patterns: Do not treat arXiv preprints as peer-reviewed evidence without checking publication status.
Do not use very broad one-word queries when a focused technical phrase is available.
Do not request very large result counts when a targeted search would answer the question.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API key?
faq.answer: No. It uses the public arXiv interface through the arxiv Python package.

faq.question: What fields are returned for each paper?
faq.answer: The script returns the paper title, abstract summary, and direct arXiv URL.

faq.question: Can it search PubMed or journal databases?
faq.answer: No. It only searches arXiv and should be combined with other tools for broader literature review.

faq.question: Are the results peer reviewed?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. arXiv hosts preprints, so users should verify publication and review status separately.

faq.question: Why might installation be required?
faq.answer: The script imports the arxiv Python package, which may not be installed in every runtime environment.

faq.question: Can I limit the number of papers?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --max-papers option to control how many results the script requests.
